description: Structured error reporting schema for the Watchdog Protocol. Agents post error reports to #openclaw-watchdog; Chief triages on a 2-hour cycle.

channel.name: #openclaw-watchdog
channel.purpose: System health alerts, agent error reports, auto-remediation logs

critical.example: All Stripe API calls failing, Mercury credentials expired

high.description: Single agent or integration degraded, Chief triages within 1 hour
high.example: Clay API returning 401, Gmail draft creation failing

medium.description: Non-blocking issue, addressed on next Chief triage cron cycle
medium.example: Enrichment data incomplete, single handoff timeout

low.description: Informational, log only for trend tracking
low.example: Transient timeout recovered on retry, minor data format mismatch

fields.agent_name: Name of the reporting agent (e.g., outreach, kit, scout)
fields.category: One of the error_categories listed above
fields.severity: One of: critical, high, medium, low
fields.tool_or_api: The specific tool or API that failed (e.g., Clay API, Stripe API, Gmail API)
fields.error_message: The actual error message or HTTP status code
fields.what_agent_was_doing: Brief description of the task or operation in progress
fields.what_is_blocked: What downstream work is blocked or degraded by this error

rules.one_report_per_error: Post one report per distinct error, not per retry attempt
rules.continue_if_possible: After reporting, continue with degraded operation if possible
rules.log_to_knowledge: Also log the error in the agent's KNOWLEDGE.md for trend tracking
rules.chief_triage: Chief reviews #openclaw-watchdog every 2 hours during business hours and posts triage summaries
